notes: Lesson 2 Orientation (act 7, 'Bayes' Theorem in Practice - the route', 1014.67s/16.9min, 7 cues rescaled to the real MP3). Long-form spiral lesson-map route (Leo/Maya dialogue) authored from the full 16988-char DB script + per-cue headline/receive/transform/pass. Reuses bayes.py idioms (prior_tree, PopGrid, bayes_spine, bayes_formula, confusion_matrix, positive_pile) so the orientation shares the visual vocabulary of the three lesson_part segments (acts 10/11/52). Review method: FAST last-frame precheck (manim -s) for all 7 cues BEFORE any full render caught 6 layout issues (obj/headline collision, stat-row crowding, stage-tag collision -> redesigned Before/During/After panel with amber focus box, positive-results label vs col-headers, safe-area overflows, takeaway/badge touch), all fixed. Then full -ql render clean first pass: every cue within pinning tolerance (delta ~-0.04s), final 1014.40s vs audio 1014.67s (delta 0.27s<0.5). READ per-cue mid frames (cue center) AND drift-free late frames extracted directly from each pinned chunk (~t=139s) to review each cue's final composed state (the render's cueNN_end.png sample lands ~0.24s into the next cue due to cumulative chunk-pinning drift, so cueNN_end.png here were replaced with the accurate chunk-late frames). Per-cue checklist (formula legibility+correctness, highlighted element matches narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via self.guard, chunk timing): all pass; iterations=1 (all fixes made at precheck). Shared concrete model with Parts 1/2/3: 100 people, prior 1%, sensitivity 99%, specificity 95%, TP1/FP5/FN0/TN94, posterior 1/6~=17%. Registers as content.orientation_video.
